metadata.preview:
# Auth refresh queue rationale
session_id: auth_refresh_session
title: Auth refresh queue rationale

## message auth_fact_1
The rejected design was one global mutex around all auth refresh work.

## message auth_fact_2
The global mutex was rejected because auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel showed unrelated tenants must not block each other.

## message auth_fact_3
RefreshQueue uses a per-tenant key: same-tenant duplicate refreshes coalesce, while different tenants continue in parallel.

parts.text: {"answer":"src/auth/queue.ts deduplicates refresh work per tenant so same-tenant duplicate refreshes coalesce, while refreshes for different tenants can continue in parallel instead of blocking each other behind one global mutex.","evidence":{"session_id":"auth_refresh_session","blob_id":"auth_refresh_session.md","message_id":"auth_fact_3"},"rationale":"The global-mutex alternative is the rejected design; the relevant final behavior is per-tenant coalescing plus cross-tenant parallelism. Billing retry uses a global mutex for provider-limit reasons, but that rationale is explicitly not for auth refresh.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["Billing retry queue rationale","Markdown parser onboarding cleanup","Auth queue rationale decoys"]}
